name: Curried Vegetable Stew with Tamarind & Coconut Milk
recipeIngredient: OR a dash of fennel seed
1 large yellow onion, sliced into 1/8-inch slices (about 1 1/4 cups)
6 whole red Thai chiles, stemmed and thinly sliced crosswise
1 large red bell pepper, seeded, cut into 1-inch thick slices
4 ounces portobellos or shiitake mushrooms, sliced 1/4 inch thick
1/2 teaspoon salt, or more to taste
1 1/2 cups cooked garbanzo beans or yellow lentils (channa dal)
2 cups snow peas or sugar snap peas, fresh or frozen
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the tamarind in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Crush with a fork and set aside to soften for 1 to 2 hours, breaking up the pulp occasionally. Use a spoon or spatula to push through the pulp through a sieve. Discard the fibrous material.
2. Measure 1/3 cup of tamarind puree and set aside.
3. Combine the spices in a small bowl and set aside.
4. Heat the oil over medium heat in a heavy, 4-quart pot with a tight-fitting lid. When hot, add the onion, ginger, garlic, and chiles, and cook, stirring until the onion is softened, about 5 minutes.
5. Add the spices and cook, stirring until fragrant, about 3 minutes. Add the red bell pepper, cauliflower, and mushrooms, and sprinkle with salt. Stir the spices and continue cooking, stirring for 5 minutes.
6. Increase the heat to high and add the tamarind puree, coconut milk, and water to the pan. Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat and simmer gently for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Add the garbanzo beans and simmer for 10 minutes until the vegetables are just tender. Add the peas and simmer for 5 minutes until they are just tender.
8. Remove the star anise. Adjust salt to taste and sprinkle with cilantro before serving.
